claim: The prompt is the fundamental unit of engineering in the age of AI agents; one well-crafted prompt can generate tens to hundreds of hours of productive work
actionable: Invest engineering time in crafting reusable, battle-tested prompt templates rather than one-off prompts
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Agentic prompts require engineering for three audiences: yourself, your team, and your agents (the stakeholder trifecta)
actionable: When writing prompts, ensure clarity for human readers (future self and teammates) and precise instructions for agent execution
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: The workflow section is the most powerful and useful part of any agentic prompt; it defines the exact steps agents execute
actionable: Focus 70% of prompt design effort on the workflow section with clear sequential or conditional steps
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Variables (both static and dynamic) in prompts reduce token waste and improve clarity by centralizing information
actionable: Define static variables (e.g., output_dir) and dynamic variables (args) once at the top; reference them throughout via consistent syntax
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Control flow prompts (conditionals, loops) are S-tier useful but require A-tier skill to design correctly
actionable: Add if/else and loop blocks to prompts when agents need to handle variable inputs; test thoroughly before production
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Delegation prompts enable agents to spawn sub-agents to parallelize work; requires careful prompt design for prompt-writing-prompts
actionable: Build primary agents that generate detailed prompts for specialized sub-agents; test prompt quality before scaling to multiple parallel agents
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Consistency in prompt format (input, workflow, output structure) reduces confusion for yourself, your team, and agents
actionable: Standardize all prompts with: metadata/title, purpose, variables, workflow, instructions, report sections; reuse sections like Lego blocks
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)

claim: Most engineers only need prompt levels 1-3 (ad-hoc, workflow, control flow) to capture 90% of agentic engineering value
actionable: Start at level 1 (simple prompts), graduate to level 3 (workflow + control flow), then add delegation or meta-prompts only if needed
source_title: Agentic Prompt Engineering with Claude Code (For you, your team, and your AGENTS)
